messages.content:
I'll start by loading the `execution-trace` skill first as required.

Now loading the `report-analyzer` skill.





I'm now downloading and analyzing the blood report PDF from the provided URL.

The PDF has been downloaded and analyzed. Based on the BloomCell clinical framework applied to the blood report, here are the 8 structured analysis objects:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "f3a1d72e-9c4b-4e8a-b016-3d2e1f5a7c90",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "HbA1c 4.8-5.6% 6.2 | Fasting Glucose 70-90 mg/dL 112 | Insulin (Fasting) 2-10 uIU/mL 18.4 | HOMA-IR <2.0 4.3",
    "group": "Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "HbA1c at 6.2% and fasting glucose at 112 mg/dL both exceed BloomCell functional ceilings (5.6% and 90 mg/dL respectively). Elevated fasting insulin (18.4 uIU/mL vs. functional max 10) and HOMA-IR of 4.3 (functional max 2.0) confirm pre-diabetic insulin resistance pattern — a high severity, high impact finding requiring immediate clinical attention.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - HbA1c Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Glucose Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum | Biomarkers.xlsx - Insulin Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "a2c8e94f-1b7d-4f3c-9e05-6a4b2d8f0c31",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "HbA1c 6.2% | Fasting Glucose 112 mg/dL | Fasting Insulin 18.4 uIU/mL | HOMA-IR 4.3 — Clinically significant insulin resistance pattern; pre-diabetic range across all four markers. Cross-referenced with elevated Triglycerides (210 mg/dL) and low HDL (38 mg/dL) confirming Metabolic Syndrome triad. Recommend immediate lifestyle and dietary intervention. Consider referral to endocrinology if no improvement within 90 days. Supportive testing: fasting C-peptide, urine microalbumin, 2-hour post-prandial glucose challenge.",
    "group": "Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "The convergence of elevated HbA1c, fasting glucose, fasting insulin, and HOMA-IR with concurrent dyslipidemia (high TG, low HDL) is a classic Metabolic Syndrome presentation per BloomCell 4-Step Analysis Logic — Step 1 (Blood Sugar). HOMA-IR of 4.3 indicates moderate-to-severe peripheral insulin resistance. The Metabolic Syndrome pattern (High Glucose + High Triglycerides + Low HDL) is explicitly flagged in BloomCell cross-marker pattern analysis. Clinical priority is high. Supportive testing triggers (C-peptide, microalbumin) sourced from Key supportive Testing sheet.",
    "referencesource": "3 M Health Check.docx - Page 2 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - HbA1c and Glucose Rows | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Insulin Resistance Row",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "b5e2f801-4d9a-4c6e-a137-9c0d3e7b1f54",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Total Cholesterol 150-199 mg/dL 238 | LDL 60-99 mg/dL 158 | Triglycerides 70-149 mg/dL 210 | HDL >60 mg/dL 38",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Total Cholesterol (238 mg/dL), LDL (158 mg/dL), and Triglycerides (210 mg/dL) all exceed BloomCell functional upper limits; HDL (38 mg/dL) falls below the functional minimum of 60 mg/dL. This lipid pattern, when combined with insulin resistance, significantly elevates cardiovascular risk.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Cholesterol Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum/High | Biomarkers.xlsx - LDL Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Triglycerides Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - HDL Row, Column Low",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "c9d4a73b-2f1e-4b80-8c52-7e5a1d3f6b08",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Your blood fat levels — including cholesterol, LDL ('bad' cholesterol), and triglycerides — are higher than the healthy functional range, while your HDL ('good' cholesterol) is lower than ideal. Think of HDL like a garbage truck that removes excess cholesterol from your arteries; when it's too low, cholesterol can build up over time. The good news is that these results are largely driven by your blood sugar imbalance (also seen in your report) and respond very well to targeted dietary and lifestyle changes. These are a snapshot in time, not a permanent condition — early action now can significantly reverse this picture within 3–6 months.",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Elevated LDL and triglycerides with low HDL in the context of insulin resistance reflects lipogenesis driven by hyperinsulinemia — excess insulin promotes hepatic triglyceride synthesis and reduces HDL clearance. Patient-friendly language modeled on BloomCell Narrative results.docx. BloomCell key recommendations flags this lipid pattern as dyslipidemia with insulin-resistance root cause, recommending dietary intervention (reduce refined carbohydrates, increase omega-3 intake) and exercise protocol.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Narrative results.docx - Page 1 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Cholesterol/LDL/Triglycerides/HDL Rows",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "e1b7c52a-8d3f-4a91-b268-4f0e9c2d5a17",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "25-OH Vitamin D3 50-80 ng/mL 18.4 | hs-CRP <1.0 mg/L 4.8 | Ferritin 50-150 ng/mL 22",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D3 at 18.4 ng/mL is critically low (BloomCell functional range: 50–80 ng/mL), representing a severity-5 deficiency directly impairing immune regulation and insulin receptor sensitivity. hs-CRP at 4.8 mg/L (functional max <1.0) signals systemic inflammation. Low ferritin (22 ng/mL vs. functional 50–150) compounds fatigue and immune compromise — together these three markers demand immediate supplementation and anti-inflammatory action within 30 days.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Vitamin D Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - hs-CRP Row, Columns Maximum/Impact Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Ferritin Row, Columns Minimum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "d6f0e318-5c2b-4d74-9a43-2b8f1e7c4d09",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start Vitamin D3 5,000 IU + K2 (MK-7 100mcg) daily with a fat-containing meal for 8 weeks, then retest. Start Iron bisglycinate 25mg every other day with Vitamin C 500mg to support ferritin repletion — avoid iron on empty stomach. Adopt an anti-inflammatory diet: eliminate refined sugars, seed oils, and processed grains; increase oily fish (sardines, salmon) 3x/week and leafy greens daily. Begin 30-minute brisk walking 5x/week immediately to improve insulin sensitivity. Request repeat hs-CRP and Vitamin D in 6–8 weeks to track acute response. Avoid high-dose iron supplementation without medical supervision if hemoglobin is normal.",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D3 deficiency at 18.4 ng/mL (>60% below functional floor of 50 ng/mL) warrants high-dose repletion per BloomCell key recommendations. K2 co-supplementation prevents calcium misallocation. Iron bisglycinate is the best-tolerated form for ferritin repletion without GI side effects per BloomCell Nutrition MVP. Vitamin C enhances non-heme iron absorption. Anti-inflammatory dietary protocol and aerobic exercise are the highest-priority interventions for concurrent insulin resistance and elevated hs-CRP per BloomCell Recovery and Nutrition MVPs — top 3 strategies by priority rank.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Vitamin D and Iron Rows | BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Inflammation Protocol | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Vitamin D/Ferritin/CRP Rows",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "a8d3b146-7e5f-4c02-b391-6c2a0d9e3f85",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Homocysteine <7 umol/L 14.8 | Vitamin B12 500-900 pg/mL 298 | Folate 10-24 ng/mL 5.2 | Magnesium 6.0-6.8 mg/dL 5.1",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Homocysteine at 14.8 umol/L (more than double BloomCell optimal threshold of <7 umol/L) alongside low B12 (298 pg/mL vs. functional 500–900), deficient folate (5.2 ng/mL vs. functional 10–24), and low magnesium (5.1 mg/dL vs. functional 6.0–6.8) constitutes a methylation insufficiency and micronutrient depletion pattern. This is a chronic systemic issue with cardiovascular, cognitive, and neurological long-term implications requiring sustained 3–6 month intervention.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Homocysteine Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - B12 Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Folate Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Magnesium Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  },
  {
    "id": "c2f9e507-3a1d-4b68-8e74-1d5b0c4f7e62",
    "date": "06/26/26 06:20 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target Homocysteine <7 umol/L, B12 >600 pg/mL, Folate >15 ng/mL, Magnesium >6.2 mg/dL, HbA1c <5.6%, LDL <100 mg/dL, Vitamin D >60 ng/mL within 6 months. Retest at 6–8 weeks: Vitamin D, hs-CRP, ferritin, homocysteine. Retest at 3 months: full lipid panel, fasting insulin, HOMA-IR, HbA1c, B12, folate. Retest at 6 months: comprehensive metabolic panel, full thyroid panel (TSH, Free T3, Free T4, Reverse T3), omega-3 index, full CBC. Introduce methylated B-Complex (methylcobalamin + methylfolate + P5P) daily long-term. Magnesium glycinate 300–400mg nightly. Consider MTHFR gene polymorphism testing if homocysteine remains elevated at 3 months. Supportive testing: organic acids test (OAT) or Genova NutrEval to assess full micronutrient and methylation status at 3-month mark. Long-term metabolic rehabilitation via progressive resistance training 3x/week to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce cardiovascular risk. Target HOMA-IR <2.0 and Triglycerides <149 mg/dL as primary 6-month metabolic success metrics.",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Methylation insufficiency (high homocysteine + low B12 + low folate) is a systemic chronic pattern requiring long-term micronutrient repletion per BloomCell key recommendations. Methylcobalamin and methylfolate bypass MTHFR conversion defects. Magnesium deficiency impairs glucose metabolism, muscle recovery, and sleep — contributing to and worsening the insulin resistance and inflammatory state. The retest schedule targets the highest-severity markers first (6–8 weeks) then full metabolic reassessment at 3 and 6 months per Master Report April 2026 long-term strategy template. Supportive testing triggers (OAT, NutrEval) sourced from Key supportive Testing sheet.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Homocysteine/B12/Methylation Rows | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Homocysteine/Magnesium Rows | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Methylation/Magnesium Rows",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  }
]
```



Now building and storing each merged record in Cosmos DB one by one.



**Storing Record 1 of 8...**✅ Record 1 saved. **Storing Record 2 of 8...**✅ Record 2 saved. **Storing Record 3 of 8...**✅ Record 3 saved. **Storing Record 4 of 8...**✅ Record 4 saved. **Storing Record 5 of 8...**✅ Record 5 saved. **Storing Record 6 of 8...**✅ Record 6 saved. **Storing Record 7 of 8...**✅ Record 7 saved. **Storing Record 8 of 8...**

8 records saved successfully.

Now updating the original document in Cosmos DB.



Document updated successfully.
